text: Maintenance Debrief Engine 85 (Fan Module / Bypass, ATA 72-20): Crew reported anomaly. Finding: Water-in-fuel (WIF) sensor triggered during pre-flight; 0.5 cc of water found in low-point drain. Turbine Rear Frame strut repair per DER-approved repair scheme RS-495-X36. NDT re-check clear. Returned to service.

Heavy Check C-Visit Finding | Engine 24 | Engine Control (FADEC): FADEC software exception code 0x4A31 — fuel control loop saturation at altitude relight attempt. Engineering disposition: Thrust reverser actuator replaced (PN: 817-C67). Full stow/deploy cycle tests × 5 — all within time limits.

Shop Finding — Engine S/N 23 | Fan Module / Bypass: Impact damage to fan spinner cone; crack propagating from rivet hole confirmed by dye-penetrant check. Disposition: Oil cooler replaced (PN: 398-X96). System pressure-tested at 90 PSI for 10 minutes — zero leak. Oil serviced and engine run.

Pilot Report — Engine 58 (Fuel System): Seal degradation resulting in higher-than-expected secondary airflow bypass; labyrinth knife-edges worn. Corrective Action: P&D valve replaced (PN: 831-C60). Engine shutdown test × 3 confirms full closure within 1.5 s. Leak check clear.

Borescope Work Order | Engine 76 | Accessory Gearbox (AGB): Fan cowl door latch pin fractured; door opened in flight causing structural damage to leading edge. Outcome: Bleed check valve replaced (PN: 274-C44). Bleed system functional test at ground idle and max continuous — normal.

Routine Inspection of Low-Pressure Compressor (LPC / Booster) on Engine 12: High-pressure fuel pump discharge pressure 200 PSI below spec at max power; pump worn. Action Taken: Performed MCD chip analysis per Engineering Order EO-612-Z78; single isolated particle cleared by metallurgy. Ops check passed.

Routine Inspection of Variable Stator Vane (VSV) System on Engine 46: Thrust reverser sleeve failed to stow fully; blocker door hinge pin corroded and seized. Action Taken: Oil cooler replaced (PN: 662-C30). System pressure-tested at 90 PSI for 10 minutes — zero leak. Oil serviced and engine run.

Heavy Check C-Visit Finding | Engine 19 | Variable Stator Vane (VSV) System: VSV unison ring wear pin fractured; complete ring replaced per AMM 72-30-12. Engineering disposition: Starter replaced (PN: 971-B83); motoring check confirmed N2 rise rate meets AMM minimum. Start cycle tested × 3.

Engine 81 AOG Recovery — Bleed Air / Anti-Ice System (ATA 36-10): Pylon secondary structure skin crack found during heavy maintenance visit; crack length 3.2 in. Expedited action: Water-in-fuel system drained and flushed per AMM 73-10-07. Filter element replaced (PN: 880-X56). WIF sensor re-tested OK.

ATA 78-30 | Engine 68: EGT margin trending -30 °C over 500 cycles; borescope confirms nozzle area growth due to tip rub. Igniter plug replaced (PN: 134-X79). Ignition system verified per AMM 74-10-00 — light-off within 10 seconds.

Non-Routine Card NRC-1011 opened for Engine 30 Oil System. Finding: Core exhaust nozzle divergent flap hinge fractured; flap found kinematically jammed. Action: T2 sensor de-iced and anti-ice inlet heater power supply verified. FADEC fault cleared. Altitude relight test successful.

ACARS Engine Alert — Engine 79 Sensor S6: Micro-cracking observed on root attachment of HPC stage-2 blades; confirmed by fluorescent penetrant inspection (FPI). Ground team response: EGT harness replaced (PN: 584-B27). All thermocouple channels checked against reference — max split 12 °C. FADEC reset.

Routine Inspection of Starter / Ignition System on Engine 18: Heavy coking and carbon buildup observed on fuel nozzle atomizers — 4 of 24 nozzles below flow spec. Action Taken: Adjusted fuel-flow metering within FADEC software limits (ref SB 74-10-73-12). Cleared for revenue service.

Post-Flight Inspection Engine 7 (ATA 73-10): Foreign Object Damage (FOD) dent found on HPT stage-2 blade leading edge; depth measured at 0.015 in. Fan cowl latch replaced (PN: 920-Z61). All 6 latches tested for positive engagement and safety catch. Ops check complete.

ATA 71-20 | Engine 14: HPC stage-7 blade tip erosion reducing surge margin below EHM threshold. Engine inlet FOD inspection completed per post-bird-strike check sheet. All blades within blend limits. Returned to service.

Borescope Work Order | Engine 85 | Starter / Ignition System: Heavy coking and carbon buildup observed on fuel nozzle atomizers — 4 of 24 nozzles below flow spec. Outcome: Performed on-wing borescope per AMM task 72-00-00-290-801. No further deterioration; component returned to service.

Heavy Check C-Visit Finding | Engine 77 | High-Pressure Turbine (HPT): FADEC software exception code 0x4A31 — fuel control loop saturation at altitude relight attempt. Engineering disposition: Component removed and replaced with serviceable unit (PN: 275-C97). Ground engine run and ops check normal.

Non-Routine Card NRC-1018 opened for Engine 10 Fan Module / Bypass. Finding: HPT stage-2 nozzle guide vanes exhibit oxidation and hot-section erosion beyond serviceable limits. Action: Anti-ice valve replaced (PN: 238-X29) and system leak-checked at 45 PSI. Functional test confirmed valve cycling.

Non-Routine Card NRC-1019 opened for Engine 45 Turbine Rear Frame (TRF). Finding: Fuel flowmeter reading 4% low vs. calibrated master; suspected sensor contamination. Action: Igniter plug replaced (PN: 635-X54). Ignition system verified per AMM 74-10-00 — light-off within 10 seconds.

Engine 85 AOG Recovery — Fan Module / Bypass (ATA 72-20): High-pressure fuel pump discharge pressure 200 PSI below spec at max power; pump worn. Expedited action: Thrust reverser actuator replaced (PN: 457-B67). Full stow/deploy cycle tests × 5 — all within time limits.

Trend Analysis Report — Engine 17 Inlet / Nacelle: Air turbine starter (ATS) drive shaft shear coupling failed during attempted engine start. Maintenance response: FADEC software updated to version 5.1.4 per SB 71-10-73-056. Ground test cycle confirms correct engine response.

Non-Routine Card NRC-1022 opened for Engine 13 Accessory Gearbox (AGB). Finding: Excessive vibration recorded on the #2 bearing structural frame — 3.2 IPS pk-pk against limit of 2.5 IPS. Action: Replaced fuel nozzle set (24 nozzles, PN: 228-Z26). Engine ground run at idle and max continuous — EGT spread within 15 °C.

Engine 22 AOG Recovery — Fuel System (ATA 73-10): ACARS transmitted unsolicited engine exceedance event; review confirmed sensor spike, not true exceedance. Expedited action: Deferred per MEL 73-10-01. Performance monitoring required every 50 flight cycles; trend reviewed at each check.

Engine 78 AOG Recovery — Turbine Rear Frame (TRF) (ATA 72-50): Bleed duct insulation blanket missing from 18-inch section; risk of overheat on adjacent hydraulic line. Expedited action: Fan acoustic liner panel repaired with structural film adhesive per AMM 71-10-04 (PN: 402-B25). Tap test confirms no further delamination.

Line Maintenance Write-Up #1025 | Inlet / Nacelle (ATA 71-10): Customer bleed air duct found cracked at flex-joint flange; hot-air leak confirmed by thermal camera. Resolution: Engine mount bushings replaced per AMM 71-20-01 (PN: 142-X74). Alignment checked; free-play within 0.008 in limit.

Borescope Work Order | Engine 66 | Fuel System: Fuel flowmeter reading 4% low vs. calibrated master; suspected sensor contamination. Outcome: Thermal camera survey performed per AMM 36-10-00; bleed air leak confirmed and duct replaced (PN: 816-B10).

Trend Analysis Report — Engine 45 High-Pressure Turbine (HPT): Combustor liner TBC spallation exposing parent metal in hot-spot region; liner at life limit. Maintenance response: Removed and routed to off-site MRO shop for full overhaul (work order WO-784-B56). Installed serviceable spare.

ATA 74-10 | Engine 3: Bleed duct insulation blanket missing from 18-inch section; risk of overheat on adjacent hydraulic line. Fan cowl latch replaced (PN: 721-C76). All 6 latches tested for positive engagement and safety catch. Ops check complete.

Auto-Alert on Sensor S8 (Engine 65). Actuator linkage found binding during manual operational check; jam nut backed off causing misalignment. Performed MCD chip analysis per Engineering Order EO-863-A82; single isolated particle cleared by metallurgy. Ops check passed.

ACARS Engine Alert — Engine 99 Sensor S6: Core exhaust nozzle divergent flap hinge fractured; flap found kinematically jammed. Ground team response: N2 sensor and wiring harness replaced (PN: 373-C63). Signal continuity confirmed on all channels. Engine run at cruise power — stable.

EHM System Flag — Sensor S20 | Engine 67 | ATA 73-20: Fan cowl door latch pin fractured; door opened in flight causing structural damage to leading edge. Field fan balance performed (4 balance weights added per AMM 72-20-09). N1 vibration reduced from 1.8 to 0.3 IPS.

Trend Analysis Report — Engine 38 High-Pressure Turbine (HPT): Sensor recalibration required: PS3 static pressure probe reading 2% high versus pitot-static reference. Maintenance response: VSV rigging adjusted per AMM 72-30-12 to restore schedule within ±1°. Functional test satisfactory.

Line Maintenance Write-Up #1033 | Accessory Gearbox (AGB) (ATA 72-60): EGT thermocouple harness chafing against fuel line; two conductors show bare wire. Resolution: Performed deep chemical wash and re-torqued casing bolts to 450 in-lbs per AMM 72-60-00-03.

ATA 74-10 | Engine 63: Rub strip contact observed on HPC stage-5 rotor; abradable coating partially displaced. Fan acoustic liner panel repaired with structural film adhesive per AMM 71-10-04 (PN: 605-X40). Tap test confirms no further delamination.

Pilot Report — Engine 3 (Accessory Gearbox (AGB)): 5th-stage bleed check valve stuck open; high-pressure bleed air back-flowing into 9th-stage duct. Corrective Action: Thrust reverser actuator replaced (PN: 585-X12). Full stow/deploy cycle tests × 5 — all within time limits.

ACARS Engine Alert — Engine 23 Sensor S2: LPT blade tip rub marks identified; rotor-to-shroud clearance measured at 0.003 in, exceeding the 0.002 in maximum. Ground team response: Oil seal replaced (PN: 779-B78). Engine oil consumption check over 10-hour period: 0.15 qt/hr — within limit of 0.4 qt/hr.

Non-Routine Card NRC-1037 opened for Engine 5 Engine Control (FADEC). Finding: Thrust reverser sleeve failed to stow fully; blocker door hinge pin corroded and seized. Action: Igniter plug replaced (PN: 471-X16). Ignition system verified per AMM 74-10-00 — light-off within 10 seconds.

Shop Finding — Engine S/N 31 | Low-Pressure Turbine (LPT): Acoustic anomaly reported by flight crew during takeoff roll; traced to combustor liner burnthrough. Disposition: P&D valve replaced (PN: 360-Z91). Engine shutdown test × 3 confirms full closure within 1.5 s. Leak check clear.

Shop Finding — Engine S/N 91 | Turbine Rear Frame (TRF): LPT blade tip rub marks identified; rotor-to-shroud clearance measured at 0.003 in, exceeding the 0.002 in maximum. Disposition: Engine mount bushings replaced per AMM 71-20-01 (PN: 707-X57). Alignment checked; free-play within 0.008 in limit.

ACARS Engine Alert — Engine 32 Sensor S11: Igniter plug A shows excessive electrode erosion; spark gap measured at 0.18 in (limit 0.12 in). Ground team response: Pylon skin crack repaired per SRM Chapter 53 approved doubler repair. NDT re-check: no crack extension. Returned to service.

EHM System Flag — Sensor S13 | Engine 95 | ATA 78-30: Igniter plug A shows excessive electrode erosion; spark gap measured at 0.18 in (limit 0.12 in). Blended out compressor blade damage per AMM 72-30-05; within all dimensional limits. FPI re-check clear.

Routine Inspection of Starter / Ignition System on Engine 86: Seal degradation resulting in higher-than-expected secondary airflow bypass; labyrinth knife-edges worn. Action Taken: T2 sensor de-iced and anti-ice inlet heater power supply verified. FADEC fault cleared. Altitude relight test successful.

Shop Finding — Engine S/N 6 | Fuel System: VSV schedule error of +3.5° at N2 = 87%; FADEC trim corrected after actuator feedback cable re-rigged. Disposition: LPT shroud segment blended within limits per AMM 72-40-07. Borescope re-check confirms crack arrest. Returned to service.

Post-Flight Inspection Engine 48 (ATA 79-00): VSV schedule error of +3.5° at N2 = 87%; FADEC trim corrected after actuator feedback cable re-rigged. N2 sensor and wiring harness replaced (PN: 620-B98). Signal continuity confirmed on all channels. Engine run at cruise power — stable.

Pilot Report — Engine 71 (High-Pressure Compressor (HPC)): P&D valve failed to close during engine shutdown; fuel dribble into combustor confirmed. Corrective Action: Thermal camera survey performed per AMM 36-10-00; bleed air leak confirmed and duct replaced (PN: 414-C95).

Pilot Report — Engine 94 (Low-Pressure Turbine (LPT)): Chip light illuminated during engine ground run; chip confirmed as single ferrous sliver <1 mm. Corrective Action: Nose cowl anti-ice duct insulation blanket replaced per AMM 30-21-01. Adjacent structure inspected — no heat damage found.

Borescope Work Order | Engine 66 | Thrust Reverser: Turbine Rear Frame strut cracks detected by eddy-current inspection; 3 of 10 struts affected. Outcome: Engine inlet FOD inspection completed per post-bird-strike check sheet. All blades within blend limits. Returned to service.

Shop Finding — Engine S/N 15 | Bleed Air / Anti-Ice System: Customer bleed air duct found cracked at flex-joint flange; hot-air leak confirmed by thermal camera. Disposition: Performed MCD chip analysis per Engineering Order EO-391-X93; single isolated particle cleared by metallurgy. Ops check passed.

Heavy Check C-Visit Finding | Engine 61 | Combustor Section: Pressure drop exceeding 15 PSI across the primary fuel manifold; filter element blocked. Engineering disposition: Engine sent to test cell for full performance restoration workscope. Post-test EGT margin: +42 °C. Returned to fleet.

Engine 4 AOG Recovery — Engine Mounts / Pylon (ATA 71-20): VSV unison ring wear pin fractured; complete ring replaced per AMM 72-30-12. Expedited action: Nose cowl anti-ice duct insulation blanket replaced per AMM 30-21-01. Adjacent structure inspected — no heat damage found.

Engine 61 AOG Recovery — Oil System (ATA 79-00): EGT margin trending -30 °C over 500 cycles; borescope confirms nozzle area growth due to tip rub. Expedited action: Oil seal replaced (PN: 415-X50). Engine oil consumption check over 10-hour period: 0.15 qt/hr — within limit of 0.4 qt/hr.

Line Maintenance Write-Up #1052 | Variable Stator Vane (VSV) System (ATA 72-30): Engine refused to accelerate above 80% N1 during takeoff; bleed air valve incorrectly rigged. Resolution: Removed and routed to off-site MRO shop for full overhaul (work order WO-155-C35). Installed serviceable spare.

Auto-Alert on Sensor S4 (Engine 53). Debris found in magnetic chip detector (MCD); metallurgical analysis confirms #3 bearing wear particles. Bleed check valve replaced (PN: 401-A35). Bleed system functional test at ground idle and max continuous — normal.

Engine 57 AOG Recovery — Starter / Ignition System (ATA 74-10): Excessive vibration N1 (1.8 IPS pk) traced to single fan blade mass imbalance; field balance performed. Expedited action: Blended out compressor blade damage per AMM 72-30-05; within all dimensional limits. FPI re-check clear.

Borescope Work Order | Engine 54 | Thrust Reverser: Oil-in-fuel indication post-maintenance; #3 bearing sump seal misassembled during last shop visit. Outcome: Combustor liner replaced with serviceable unit (PN: 654-X78). Borescope post-installation confirms correct seating.

Borescope Work Order | Engine 16 | Low-Pressure Turbine (LPT): Engine refused to accelerate above 80% N1 during takeoff; bleed air valve incorrectly rigged. Outcome: Performed MCD chip analysis per Engineering Order EO-723-X62; single isolated particle cleared by metallurgy. Ops check passed.

Trend Analysis Report — Engine 3 Oil System: Hydraulic actuator seal failure causing hydraulic fluid contamination in nacelle zone 3. Maintenance response: Engine mount bushings replaced per AMM 71-20-01 (PN: 558-B61). Alignment checked; free-play within 0.008 in limit.

Engine 48 AOG Recovery — Variable Stator Vane (VSV) System (ATA 72-30): Oil filter bypass indicator (OFBI) popped in-flight; filter element found heavily loaded with metallic debris. Expedited action: Fan cowl latch replaced (PN: 376-C60). All 6 latches tested for positive engagement and safety catch. Ops check complete.

Maintenance Debrief Engine 94 (Oil System, ATA 79-00): Crew reported anomaly. Finding: Fan cowl door latch pin fractured; door opened in flight causing structural damage to leading edge. FADEC software updated to version 5.1.4 per SB 79-00-73-056. Ground test cycle confirms correct engine response.

Trend Analysis Report — Engine 19 Fan Module / Bypass: N2 speed sensor signal intermittent above FL350; EMI source identified on P-lead harness. Maintenance response: EGT harness replaced (PN: 152-B11). All thermocouple channels checked against reference — max split 12 °C. FADEC reset.

Pilot Report — Engine 94 (Starter / Ignition System): Sensor recalibration required: PS3 static pressure probe reading 2% high versus pitot-static reference. Corrective Action: FADEC software updated to version 5.1.4 per SB 74-10-73-056. Ground test cycle confirms correct engine response.

Maintenance Debrief Engine 63 (Variable Stator Vane (VSV) System, ATA 72-30): Crew reported anomaly. Finding: Hot streak on turbine inlet temperature (TIT) sensor trace indicates circumferential non-uniformity in combustion. Fan acoustic liner panel repaired with structural film adhesive per AMM 71-10-04 (PN: 847-C44). Tap test confirms no further delamination.

EHM System Flag — Sensor S3 | Engine 99 | ATA 72-20: Air turbine starter (ATS) drive shaft shear coupling failed during attempted engine start. Nose cowl anti-ice duct insulation blanket replaced per AMM 30-21-01. Adjacent structure inspected — no heat damage found.

Auto-Alert on Sensor S1 (Engine 67). Oil cooler matrix found cracked at inlet header, causing low oil pressure indication. Carried out compressor on-wing wash per CFMI SB 72-0XXX. EGT margin recovered by 18 °C. Returned to service.

Shop Finding — Engine S/N 15 | Low-Pressure Turbine (LPT): P&D valve failed to close during engine shutdown; fuel dribble into combustor confirmed. Disposition: Fan cowl latch replaced (PN: 720-B55). All 6 latches tested for positive engagement and safety catch. Ops check complete.

Non-Routine Card NRC-1066 opened for Engine 25 Oil System. Finding: Rub strip contact observed on HPC stage-5 rotor; abradable coating partially displaced. Action: Anti-ice valve replaced (PN: 295-A48) and system leak-checked at 45 PSI. Functional test confirmed valve cycling.

Auto-Alert on Sensor S19 (Engine 35). Hydraulic actuator seal failure causing hydraulic fluid contamination in nacelle zone 3. EGT harness replaced (PN: 320-X39). All thermocouple channels checked against reference — max split 12 °C. FADEC reset.

Maintenance Debrief Engine 7 (Turbine Rear Frame (TRF), ATA 72-50): Crew reported anomaly. Finding: Oil consumption trend exceeding 0.5 qt/hr; on-wing investigation reveals #4 bearing carbon seal degradation. Igniter plug replaced (PN: 409-B57). Ignition system verified per AMM 74-10-00 — light-off within 10 seconds.

Non-Routine Card NRC-1069 opened for Engine 70 Fan Module / Bypass. Finding: LPC booster blade erosion causing inlet distortion and increased N1 vibration. Action: Performed deep chemical wash and re-torqued casing bolts to 450 in-lbs per AMM 72-20-00-03.

Trend Analysis Report — Engine 81 Engine Mounts / Pylon: EGT margin trending -30 °C over 500 cycles; borescope confirms nozzle area growth due to tip rub. Maintenance response: VSV rigging adjusted per AMM 72-30-12 to restore schedule within ±1°. Functional test satisfactory.

EHM System Flag — Sensor S6 | Engine 100 | ATA 73-10: In-flight deployment warning light illuminated; reverser cowl latch safety switch found open-circuit. Sensor recalibrated against master gauge (serial #721-X16). Ground engine run at max continuous power — all params normal.

Borescope Work Order | Engine 66 | Engine Mounts / Pylon: Inter-turbine temperature (ITT) exceedance recorded — peak of 987 °C against a redline of 960 °C during climb. Outcome: Igniter plug replaced (PN: 561-X32). Ignition system verified per AMM 74-10-00 — light-off within 10 seconds.

Heavy Check C-Visit Finding | Engine 61 | Turbine Rear Frame (TRF): Air turbine starter (ATS) drive shaft shear coupling failed during attempted engine start. Engineering disposition: N2 sensor and wiring harness replaced (PN: 495-C57). Signal continuity confirmed on all channels. Engine run at cruise power — stable.

Maintenance Debrief Engine 54 (Exhaust / Nozzle, ATA 78-30): Crew reported anomaly. Finding: Oil filter bypass indicator (OFBI) popped in-flight; filter element found heavily loaded with metallic debris. Nose cowl anti-ice duct insulation blanket replaced per AMM 30-21-01. Adjacent structure inspected — no heat damage found.

Pilot Report — Engine 40 (Thrust Reverser): Debris found in magnetic chip detector (MCD); metallurgical analysis confirms #3 bearing wear particles. Corrective Action: Adjusted fuel-flow metering within FADEC software limits (ref SB 78-30-73-12). Cleared for revenue service.

Routine Inspection of Combustor Section on Engine 96: Fan blade dovetail fretting wear observed; fits within blend limits per AMM 72-20-03. Action Taken: Adjusted fuel-flow metering within FADEC software limits (ref SB 72-40-73-12). Cleared for revenue service.

Pilot Report — Engine 82 (Oil System): FADEC software exception code 0x4A31 — fuel control loop saturation at altitude relight attempt. Corrective Action: High-pressure fuel pump replaced (PN: 116-C32). Discharge pressure confirmed at 2,950 PSI at max continuous — within spec.

Line Maintenance Write-Up #1079 | Bleed Air / Anti-Ice System (ATA 36-10): AGB oil filler cap found improperly seated; oil loss estimated at 2.3 qt over 4-hour flight. Resolution: Removed and routed to off-site MRO shop for full overhaul (work order WO-622-X11). Installed serviceable spare.

Non-Routine Card NRC-1080 opened for Engine 81 Bleed Air / Anti-Ice System. Finding: VSV unison ring wear pin fractured; complete ring replaced per AMM 72-30-12. Action: Thermal camera survey performed per AMM 36-10-00; bleed air leak confirmed and duct replaced (PN: 510-Z61).

Pilot Report — Engine 10 (Low-Pressure Compressor (LPC / Booster)): FADEC logged 14 EICAS maintenance messages relating to T4.5 thermocouple channel mismatch. Corrective Action: Performed on-wing borescope per AMM task 72-00-00-290-801. No further deterioration; component returned to service.

Engine 82 AOG Recovery — Low-Pressure Turbine (LPT) (ATA 72-40): Fan blade dovetail fretting wear observed; fits within blend limits per AMM 72-20-03. Expedited action: Engine sent to test cell for full performance restoration workscope. Post-test EGT margin: +42 °C. Returned to fleet.

Auto-Alert on Sensor S3 (Engine 16). Fuel nozzle #12 flow rate at 87% of nominal; nozzle pulled and replaced. FADEC software updated to version 5.1.4 per SB 72-20-73-056. Ground test cycle confirms correct engine response.
